wheel_segments.label: Digital Arrest

real_case.name: Ramesh Gupta
real_case.what_happened: Got call from CBI Inspector Sharma. Told his Aadhaar was linked to drug money. Kept on video call for 6 hours. Transferred Rs 14 lakh to government escrow. Lost entire retirement savings.

wheel_segments.how_it_works: Scammer poses as CBI or ED officer on video call. Claims your Aadhaar is linked to crime. Keeps you on call for hours using psychological pressure. Demands money to clear your name.
red_flags: CBI never arrests anyone digitally — no such law exists in India
Real agencies send official written notices only
Any caller who says dont tell family is manipulating you
Government never takes payment on UPI or NEFT
wheel_segments.safety_tip: Hang up immediately. Call 1930. Tell your family right away.

wheel_segments.label: UPI Trap

real_case.name: Sunita Sharma
real_case.what_happened: Received Rs 1 credit from unknown UPI ID. Got call saying Amazon refund of Rs 1299 needs OTP verification. Shared OTP. Rs 38000 debited from account within seconds.

wheel_segments.how_it_works: Scammer credits Rs 1 to your account to gain trust. Then calls saying you need to share OTP to receive a larger refund. But OTP actually authorises them to debit your full account balance.
red_flags: You NEVER need OTP to receive money — ever
Rs 1 credit is bait to make the scam feel real
Legitimate refunds are automatic — no call or OTP needed
Time pressure 10 minutes to expire is manipulation
wheel_segments.safety_tip: OTP only sends money OUT of your account. Never share OTP to receive money.

wheel_segments.label: OTP Scam
wheel_segments.today_stat: Most common scam — 4,521 cases last month

real_case.name: Mohan Lal
real_case.what_happened: Got call from SBI officer. Told account would be blocked. Asked for OTP to verify. Shared 3 OTPs in panic. Rs 1.2 lakh transferred in 3 separate transactions.

wheel_segments.how_it_works: Scammer poses as bank employee. Creates urgency that account will be blocked or KYC expired. Asks for OTP to verify. Each OTP they receive authorises a separate bank transfer.
red_flags: Banks NEVER ask for OTP — this is their official policy
Real bank calls come from toll-free numbers only
Account block threats are designed to cause panic
Never share OTP with anyone including bank staff
wheel_segments.safety_tip: Hang up. Call bank directly on the number printed on your debit card.
wheel_segments.source: RBI Annual Report on Cyber Fraud 2024

wheel_segments.label: Investment Trap
wheel_segments.today_stat: 2,341 fake SEBI groups shut down this year

real_case.name: Priya Nair
real_case.what_happened: Joined WhatsApp investment group. Got real Rs 12000 profit in first month. Invested Rs 4.5 lakh in month 2. Group disappeared overnight. Money completely gone.

wheel_segments.how_it_works: Fake SEBI certified traders show fabricated profit screenshots. Give small real profits initially to build trust. After trust is built victim invests large amount which completely disappears.
red_flags: SEBI prohibits guaranteed return promises — it is illegal
Real investments always carry risk — no exceptions ever
Screenshots of profits are trivially easy to fake
Pressure to invest more after initial small gains
wheel_segments.safety_tip: Verify any advisor at sebi.gov.in before investing a single rupee.

wheel_segments.label: Deepfake Call
wheel_segments.today_stat: AI voice cloning attacks up 156% in 2024

real_case.name: Kavitha Menon
real_case.what_happened: Received WhatsApp video call from son number. Son face and voice said he was detained at Dubai airport and needed Rs 87000 immediately. She transferred money. Son was in Bengaluru the whole time.

wheel_segments.how_it_works: AI tools can clone a voice from just 3 seconds of audio from social media videos. Scammers create convincing video calls using deepfake technology targeting elderly parents.
red_flags: Lip sync is slightly delayed in AI generated video
Any request for money plus secrecy is always a red flag
Real emergency means real person accepts a callback
Hair edges and facial boundaries look slightly unnatural
wheel_segments.safety_tip: Hang up. Call back on the real saved number. Ask a personal question only they would know.

wheel_segments.label: Fake Job
wheel_segments.today_stat: Peak placement season — targeting freshers right now

real_case.name: Arjun Patel
real_case.what_happened: Got WhatsApp message with Infosys offer letter. 6 LPA package. Paid Rs 2500 document fee then Rs 8000 training fee then Rs 15000 background check then Rs 19500 laptop deposit. Total Rs 45000 lost. Never joined.

wheel_segments.how_it_works: Fake offer letters with real company logos. Starts with small fee of Rs 2000 to 3000. Builds trust then asks for more fees. Multiple escalating charges until victim stops paying.
red_flags: Real companies NEVER charge any fees for joining
Infosys TCS Wipro HR use company email not Gmail
No legitimate company asks for UPI payment from candidates
Offer without any interview process is always suspicious
wheel_segments.safety_tip: Verify jobs only on official company career portals. Never pay any fees to get a job.

wheel_segments.label: Lottery Scam
wheel_segments.today_stat: 12,450 cases — highest in rural areas of India

real_case.name: Bharti Devi
real_case.what_happened: Got SMS saying KBC Season 15 lucky winner Rs 25 lakh prize. Called the number. Paid Rs 5000 GST then Rs 8000 legal fee then Rs 11000 customs fee then Rs 8000 more fees. After Rs 32000 total she realized it was fraud.

wheel_segments.how_it_works: Fake KBC or government lottery SMS. Asks for small processing fee first. Each payment generates a new excuse for another fee. Victim keeps paying hoping to eventually receive the prize.
red_flags: You cannot win a lottery you never entered — impossible
KBC selects contestants only through official registration
Any prize that requires upfront payment is always fraud
Government schemes never ask for GST or fees via SMS
wheel_segments.safety_tip: Block and report the number immediately. No legitimate prize ever requires upfront payment.

wheel_segments.label: KYC Fraud
wheel_segments.today_stat: Paytm and HDFC KYC scam links spreading on WhatsApp now

real_case.name: Deepak Mishra
real_case.what_happened: Got SMS with Paytm KYC link. Clicked the link and it looked exactly like Paytm website. Entered Aadhaar and OTP on the fake site. Rs 67000 from linked bank account transferred within minutes.

wheel_segments.how_it_works: Fake KYC websites that look identical to real apps. SMS with link claims account will be blocked. Victim enters Aadhaar plus OTP on fake site. Scammer uses these to complete fraudulent transactions.
red_flags: Paytm KYC is always done inside the official app only
Check URL carefully — paytm-kyc.tk is NOT paytm.com
Never enter Aadhaar on any link received via SMS
Real KYC never needs OTP and Aadhaar together
wheel_segments.safety_tip: KYC is only done inside official apps. Never click SMS links for KYC updates.
